name: Recursion Intro
description: Recursion solves a problem by reducing it to smaller instances of the same problem.
exampleJava: void solve(int n) { if (n == 0) return; solve(n - 1); }
commonMistakes: Forgetting to reduce the problem size
Calling the same function with unchanged parameters

name: Base Case
description: A base case stops recursion and prevents infinite calls.
commonMistakes: Missing the stopping condition
Writing a base case that never matches

name: Parameterized Recursion
description: Parameterized recursion carries the answer forward using arguments.
exampleJava: void sum(int i, int acc) { if (i == 0) return; sum(i - 1, acc + i); }
commonMistakes: Mutating the wrong accumulator
Not updating parameters before the next call

name: Functional Recursion
description: Functional recursion returns a value from smaller subproblems and combines the results.
exampleJava: int fact(int n) { return n <= 1 ? 1 : n * fact(n - 1); }
commonMistakes: Forgetting to return the recursive result
Combining values in the wrong order

name: Recursion on Strings
description: Strings can be processed recursively by shrinking the active range or prefix.
exampleJava: return s.charAt(l) == s.charAt(r) && isPal(s, l + 1, r - 1);
commonMistakes: Using the wrong stopping indices
Not handling empty or single-character strings

name: Recursion on Arrays
description: Arrays can be processed recursively by moving an index or splitting the range.
commonMistakes: Stepping past array bounds
Not updating the index

name: Tree Recursion
description: Tree recursion makes more than one recursive call from a single state.
commonMistakes: Ignoring the exponential growth of calls
Missing one of the base cases

name: Backtracking Basics
description: Backtracking explores a choice, recurses, then undoes the choice before trying the next path.
commonMistakes: Forgetting to undo the last choice
Sharing mutable state incorrectly

name: Subsequence Generation
description: At each index, decide whether to include or exclude the current element.
commonMistakes: Missing one branch of the decision tree
Appending answers too early

name: Permutations
description: Permutations recursively place unused elements into the current position.
commonMistakes: Reusing an element without marking it
Not restoring used state on backtrack

name: Memoization
description: Memoization stores results of repeated recursive states to avoid recomputation.
commonMistakes: Caching under the wrong key
Not initializing the memo table properly

name: Divide and Conquer
description: Divide-and-conquer splits a problem into subranges, solves them recursively, then merges the answers.
exampleJava: int mid = (l + r) / 2; solve(l, mid); solve(mid + 1, r);
commonMistakes: Wrong range boundaries
Forgetting the merge/combine step

name: Recursive Search
description: Recursive search explores a state space with pruning and validity checks.
commonMistakes: Not pruning impossible states
Failing to restore state after exploration
